author: Sofia Martínez
title: Build E-E-A-T Signals That Actually Move Google Rankings in 2026
description: Google's E-E-A-T framework now directly impacts rankings. Learn exactly which signals matter most and how to implement them for your SMB in the next 90 days.

sections.p: We've watched E-E-A-T evolve from a vague quality signal into Google's core ranking filter. By mid-2025, sites ignoring it saw 15-30% traffic drops in competitive verticals. The shift isn't subtle: Google now requires demonstrable Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness before your content ranks at all, especially for YMYL categories like health, finance, and legal services. If you run a service business in these spaces, E-E-A-T isn't optional—it's your baseline.

sections.h2: Experience: Show Real Work, Not Just Claims

sections.p: Experience is the signal most SMBs miss. Google doesn't just want to know you exist—it wants proof you've actually done the thing you're ranking for. For a physical therapy clinic, that means detailed case studies showing patient outcomes. For a tax prep service, it's specific examples of tax scenarios you've handled and solved. Generic 'we've been in business 20 years' doesn't cut it anymore.

Here's what works: Create 4-6 detailed case studies per quarter showing before-and-after results. Include metrics—not vague language like 'client felt better,' but 'client regained 40% ROM in 8 weeks' or 'reduced tax liability by $12,400.' Add the client's name and photo (with permission). Google's algorithm now weights first-hand experience signals 40% higher than general expertise claims.

list: Create detailed author bios on every content piece—include certifications, years in field, specific accomplishments
Publish 8-12 in-depth guides annually under your expert's name, not the company generic blog
Get mentioned in niche industry publications (even small trade journals count—quality over reach)
Add structured data markup for author credentials using Schema.org CreativeWork and Person types
Link to your expert's LinkedIn profile with 500+ connections and actual endorsements in the service category

sections.p: The author-centric approach matters because Google now treats content credibility as inseparable from the person behind it. A 2,000-word article on 'rotator cuff repair recovery' written by Dr. Sarah Chen (published under her name, with her credentials visible) outranks a similar article attributed to 'ABC Physical Therapy Team.' Assign every piece to a real expert, show their education, and update their profile across your site quarterly.

sections.h2: Authoritativeness: Citations, Reviews, and Topical Dominance

sections.p: Authoritativeness is built through three specific channels: external citations, customer reviews, and topical depth. External citations mean being mentioned in industry publications, getting quoted, or being listed on industry associations. If you run a solar installation company, getting cited on EnergySage or mentioned in a Solar Power & Design article counts more than 10 local directory listings.

list: Customer reviews: Target 15+ verified reviews per quarter on Google, Trustpilot, and niche-specific platforms (Angi for contractors, Vitals for healthcare)
Industry mentions: Pitch story ideas to trade publications in your vertical—get 2-3 mentions annually minimum
Topical depth: Create a complete content hub around your core service area (30+ interconnected pieces minimum), not scattered one-offs
Certification badges: Display relevant certifications prominently (industry body logos, safety certifications, insurance validations)

sections.quote: E-E-A-T isn't about looking authoritative—it's about proving you are. Every citation, review, and piece of content is a cumulative signal. One 5-star review matters less than 50 genuine ones with detailed feedback.

sections.h2: Trustworthiness: Security, Privacy, and Transparent Operations

sections.p: This is where most SMBs stumble. Trustworthiness requires visible security (HTTPS everywhere), privacy policy clarity, transparent pricing, and accessible contact information. Google's 2025 algorithms now check: Is your SSL certificate valid? Can users easily verify your business address? Do you clearly state refund policies? Is customer data protected? A single security warning will tank E-E-A-T credibility across your entire domain.

Actionable steps: Audit your site for mixed content warnings (HTTPS on some pages, HTTP on others—fix immediately). Update your privacy policy quarterly to reflect actual data practices. Add a 'Trust & Security' footer section with your business license number, insurance details, and membership in relevant professional bodies. For service businesses, add a 'Meet the Team' page with background checks verification visible. These micro-signals compound into 20-35% improved CTR from search results within 60 days of implementation.
